questions.q: In which century did Aryabhata live and work?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was a 5th-century CE mathematician-astronomer, born in 476 CE.

questions.q: What is the name of Aryabhata's most famous work?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's principal surviving work is the Aryabhatiya, composed when he was 23 years old.

questions.q: Aryabhata was primarily known as a mathematician and which other kind of scientist?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata was a mathematician-astronomer whose Aryabhatiya covers both mathematics and astronomy.

questions.q: What revolutionary idea about the Earth did Aryabhata propose?
options: The Earth is flat and stationary
The Earth is at the center of the universe and motionless
The Earth rotates on its own axis
questions.explanation: Aryabhata proposed that the Earth rotates on its axis, explaining the apparent daily motion of the stars.

questions.q: Aryabhata gave a famous approximation for which mathematical constant?
options: Pi (the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter)
questions.explanation: Aryabhata gave an accurate approximation of pi, stating it as approximately 3.1416.

questions.q: India's first satellite, launched in 1975, was named after which scientist?
questions.explanation: India's first satellite, launched in 1975, was named Aryabhata in his honor.

questions.q: Which numeral system feature is associated with the Indian mathematics tradition Aryabhata worked within?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's work reflects the Indian place-value system that underlies modern decimal notation.

questions.q: Aryabhata is regarded as one of the great scientists of which ancient civilization?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata is celebrated as a leading figure of classical Indian mathematics and astronomy.

questions.q: What did Aryabhata correctly identify as the cause of eclipses?
options: A demon swallowing the Sun or Moon
Shadows cast by the Earth and Moon
questions.explanation: Aryabhata explained eclipses scientifically as shadows: the Moon's shadow in solar eclipses and the Earth's shadow in lunar eclipses.

questions.q: Aryabhata explained that the Moon and planets shine because of what?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata stated that the Moon and planets shine by reflecting sunlight rather than by their own light.

questions.q: In what year was Aryabhata born?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata states in the Aryabhatiya that he was 23 years old in 499 CE, placing his birth in 476 CE.

questions.q: The Aryabhatiya is traditionally said to have been composed in or around which year?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata wrote that he was 23 in the year corresponding to 499 CE, the date associated with the Aryabhatiya.

questions.q: Which city, an ancient center of learning, is strongly associated with Aryabhata's life and work?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata is associated with Kusumapura, near Pataliputra (modern Patna), a major center of learning at the time.

questions.q: Approximately what value of pi did Aryabhata give in the Aryabhatiya?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata described a procedure yielding pi as approximately 3.1416, remarkably accurate for the period.

questions.q: Aryabhata's description of pi famously hinted at which property of the constant?
options: That it is a whole number
That it changes for different circles
That it is 'approximate' (asanna), suggesting it is not an exact ratio
questions.explanation: Aryabhata called his value of pi 'approximate' (asanna), which some scholars see as an early hint of its irrationality.

questions.q: The four sections (padas) of the Aryabhatiya include the Ganitapada, which deals with what?
questions.explanation: The Ganitapada is the mathematics section of the Aryabhatiya, covering arithmetic, algebra, and geometry.

questions.q: Aryabhata's work contains an important early table of which trigonometric quantity?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata produced a table of sine (jya) values, foundational to the development of trigonometry.

questions.q: The English word 'sine' is generally traced back, via Arabic, to which Sanskrit term used by Aryabhata?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit 'jya/jiva' was transliterated into Arabic and later mistranslated into Latin as 'sinus', giving 'sine'.

questions.q: Aryabhata gave correct formulas for the sum of which mathematical series?
options: Squares and cubes of the natural numbers
questions.explanation: Aryabhata gave formulas for summing series of natural numbers and their squares and cubes.

questions.q: Aryabhata used a unique alphabetic notation to represent large numbers. What is this system commonly called?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata devised an alphabetic system of numeration assigning numerical values to Sanskrit syllables for compact representation.

questions.q: Aryabhata's value for the length of the sidereal day (one rotation relative to the stars) was notably close to which modern figure?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's estimate of the sidereal day (about 23 hours 56 minutes) is remarkably close to the modern value.

questions.q: Which later Indian mathematician wrote an influential commentary (Aryabhatiya Bhasya) on Aryabhata's work?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara I, a 7th-century mathematician, wrote a major commentary on the Aryabhatiya and helped spread its ideas.

questions.q: Aryabhata gave a general method for solving which type of equation, foundational to Indian algebra?
options: Indeterminate linear (first-degree) equations, via the kuttaka method
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's kuttaka ('pulverizer') method solves indeterminate linear equations, a key contribution to number theory and algebra.

questions.q: Aryabhata's astronomical model explained the apparent daily westward motion of the stars by attributing motion to what?
options: The rotating sphere of fixed stars
The rotation of the Earth itself
questions.explanation: Aryabhata reasoned that the Earth's rotation, not a moving heaven, produces the apparent daily motion of the stars.

questions.q: Roughly what value did Aryabhata give for the circumference of the Earth?
options: About 4,967 yojanas (close to the true value)
He gave no estimate of Earth's size
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's Earth circumference of about 4,967 yojanas converts to a value close to the modern measurement.

questions.q: Which pada of the Aryabhatiya, named for the 'reckoning of time', gives methods for finding planetary positions for a given day and handling the intercalary month?
questions.explanation: The Kalakriyapada ('reckoning of time') covers everyday units of time, the calculation of planetary positions for a given day, and the intercalary month.

questions.q: The Golapada section of the Aryabhatiya is devoted to which subject?
options: The celestial sphere and spherical astronomy
questions.explanation: The Golapada ('on the sphere') treats the celestial sphere, spherical trigonometry, and the geometry of the heavens.

questions.q: Aryabhata's astronomical ideas influenced scholars in the Islamic world after his works were translated into which language?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's astronomy reached the Islamic world through Arabic translations, influencing later mathematics and astronomy.

questions.q: Aryabhata's planetary model is sometimes described as containing an early form of which concept used to explain planetary motion?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata's geometric planetary model used epicycles and eccentrics to account for the observed motions of the planets.

questions.q: The opening section of the Aryabhatiya, which compactly lists astronomical constants in verse, is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Gitikapada, also called Dasagitika, opens the Aryabhatiya with a concise set of astronomical parameters in verse.
